{{日本の古墳 |名称 =塚穴古墳 |画像 = |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[長野町 (大阪府)#上原西町|上原西町]]19 |位置 = |緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = |経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |ISO = |形状 = 現状で[[円墳]] |規模 = 現状で約15m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = 不明 |被葬者 = 不明（[[高向王]]との説あり<ref name="sankei" />） |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = 解体後に近世に再構築 |地図 = |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }}
'''塚穴古墳'''（つかあなこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[長野町 (大阪府)#上原西町|上原西町]]にある[[古墳]]。現状の形状は[[円墳]]。
== 概要 == もともと[[古墳時代]]に作られた古墳であるが、一旦壊されたのちに石室が再構築されており、このような経過をもつ古墳は類を見ない<ref name="bunkazai">上原土地区画整理に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書（上原遺跡 塚穴古墳）（河内長野市教育委員会、1998年）</ref>。
河内長野市教育委員会の調査では、石室内部からは人の火葬骨の入った[[甕]]が出土したほか、土器や陶磁器に混じって[[永楽通宝]]や[[寛永通宝]]などの銅銭も出土していること、周辺の[[宝篋印塔]]などの石造物に刻まれた年号などから、古墳の石室は江戸時代に組み直されて、墓地に転用されたとみられている<ref name="bunkazai" />
== 伝承 == 地元では、「[[豊臣秀吉]]による[[大坂城]]築城の際に、石材を得るために古墳を解体した。あまりに大きな石だったので[[くさび]]を入れて割り、運び出そうとしたが『怪異が生じ』、元のように石室を組み直した」との伝承がある<ref name="sankei">[https://www.sankei.com/west/news/170923/wst1709230011-n1.html 中身を暴こうとすると天変地異が起きた?「塚穴古墳」の怪伝説] [[産経新聞]] 2017年9月13日付配信記事</ref>。 実際に石室内の石材には、石を割るためのくさびの痕が確認できる。
==参考文献== * 上原土地区画整理に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書（上原遺跡 塚穴古墳）（河内長野市教育委員会、1998年） * 河内長野市史 1巻（1994年）<ref name="sankei" /> * 現地設置看板（河内長野市上原財産区、1997年7月）
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
{{デフォルトソート:つかあなこふん かわちなかのし}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:河内長野市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Eiraku Tsūhō|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Eiraku Tsūhō|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 http:// -> https:// (web.archive.org) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =塚穴古墳 |画像 =[[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション =塚穴古墳全景 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|上原西町]]19 |位置 = |緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = |経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |ISO = |形状 = 現状で[[円墳]] |規模 = 現状で約15m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = 不明 |被葬者 = 不明（[[高向王]]との説あり<ref name="sankei" />） |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = 解体後に近世に再構築 |地図 = |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''塚穴古墳'''（つかあなこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|上原西町]]にある[[古墳]]。現状の形状は[[円墳]]。
== 概要 == もともと[[古墳時代]]に作られた古墳であるが、一旦壊されたのちに[[石室]]が再構築されており、このような経過をもつ古墳は類を見ない<ref name="bunkazai">上原土地区画整理に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書（上原遺跡 塚穴古墳）（河内長野市教育委員会、1998年）</ref>。
河内長野市教育委員会の調査では、石室内部からは人の火葬骨が入った[[甕]]が出土したほか、土器や陶磁器に混じって[[永楽通宝]]や[[寛永通宝]]などの銅銭も出土していること、周辺の[[宝篋印塔]]などの石造物に刻まれた年号などから、古墳の石室は[[江戸時代]]に組み直されて、墓地に転用されたとみられている<ref name="bunkazai" />。
== 伝承 == 地元では、「[[豊臣秀吉]]による[[大坂城]]築城の際に、石材を得るために古墳を解体した。あまりに大きな石だったので[[くさび]]を入れて割り、運び出そうとしたが『怪異が生じ』、元のように石室を組み直した」との伝承がある<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|和書|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=中身を暴こうとすると天変地異が起きた?「塚穴古墳」の怪伝説|newspaper=産経ニュース|publisher=[[産経デジタル]]|date=2017年9月13日|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=2024年4月22日}}</ref>。 実際に石室内の石材には、石を割るためのくさびの痕が確認できる。
==参考文献== * 上原土地区画整理に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書（上原遺跡 塚穴古墳）（河内長野市教育委員会、1998年） * 河内長野市史 1巻（1994年）<ref name="sankei" /> * 現地設置看板（河内長野市上原財産区、1997年7月）
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
{{デフォルトソート:つかあなこふん かわちなかのし}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[category:河内長野市の歴史]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)}}[[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)}}[[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)}}[[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)}}[[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)}}[[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano City)}}[[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.
