{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alias = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map2 = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaan Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} to procure stones. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
