'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |icon = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and artifacts suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |label = |label_position = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original form<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local informational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |lat_deg = |lat_min = |lat_sec = |long_deg = |long_min = |long_sec = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.
