'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = [[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}} 19, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|ja|永楽通宝|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|ja|寛永通宝|12=draft}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大坂城|12=draft}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =塚穴古墳 |画像 =[[File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)１.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション =塚穴古墳全景 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|上原西町]]19 |位置 = |緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = |経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |ISO = |形状 = 現状で[[円墳]] |規模 = 現状で約15m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = 不明 |被葬者 = 不明（[[高向王]]との説あり<ref name="sankei" />） |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = 解体後に近世に再構築 |地図 = |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''塚穴古墳'''（つかあなこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|上原西町]]にある[[古墳]]。現状の形状は[[円墳]]。
== 概要 == もともと[[古墳時代]]に作られた古墳であるが、一旦壊されたのちに[[石室]]が再構築されており、このような経過をもつ古墳は類を見ない<ref name="bunkazai">上原土地区画整理に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書（上原遺跡 塚穴古墳）（河内長野市教育委員会、1998年）</ref>。
河内長野市教育委員会の調査では、石室内部からは人の火葬骨が入った[[甕]]が出土したほか、土器や陶磁器に混じって[[永楽通宝]]や[[寛永通宝]]などの銅銭も出土していること、周辺の[[宝篋印塔]]などの石造物に刻まれた年号などから、古墳の石室は[[江戸時代]]に組み直されて、墓地に転用されたとみられている<ref name="bunkazai" />。
== 伝承 == 地元では、「[[豊臣秀吉]]による[[大坂城]]築城の際に、石材を得るために古墳を解体した。あまりに大きな石だったので[[くさび]]を入れて割り、運び出そうとしたが『怪異が生じ』、元のように石室を組み直した」との伝承がある<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|和書|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=中身を暴こうとすると天変地異が起きた?「塚穴古墳」の怪伝説|newspaper=産経ニュース|publisher=[[産経デジタル]]|date=2017年9月13日|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=2024年4月22日}}</ref>。 実際に石室内の石材には、石を割るためのくさびの痕が確認できる。
==参考文献== * 上原土地区画整理に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書（上原遺跡 塚穴古墳）（河内長野市教育委員会、1998年） * 河内長野市史 1巻（1994年）<ref name="sankei" /> * 現地設置看板（河内長野市上原財産区、1997年7月）
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
{{デフォルトソート:つかあなこふん かわちなかのし}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[category:河内長野市の歴史]]

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|qq=|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

== Overview ==
Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

== Legend ==
Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

== References ==
* Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.
* ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" />
* Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|qq=|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

== Overview ==
Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

== Legend ==
Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

== References ==
* Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.
* ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" />
* Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

== Overview ==
Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

== Legend ==
Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

== References ==
* Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.
* ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" />
* Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

== Overview ==
Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

== Legend ==
Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

== References ==
* Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.
* ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" />
* Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)2.jpg |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572}} |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu no Ō|1=Takamuku no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28415200}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

== Overview ==
Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

== Legend ==
Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

== References ==
* Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.
* ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" />
* Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

== Overview ==
Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

== Legend ==
Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

== References ==
* Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.
* ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" />
* Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

== Overview ==
Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

== Legend ==
Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

== References ==
* Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.
* ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" />
* Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

== Overview ==
Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.

Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.

== Legend ==
Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)2.jpg |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572}} |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu no Ō|1=Takamuku no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28415200}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)2.jpg |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572}} |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu no Ō|1=Takamuku no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28415200}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]], {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as [[:en:Yongle Tongbao|Yongle Tongbao]] and [[:en:Kan'ei Tsūhō|Kan'ei Tsūhō]]. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] under [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|en|Yongle Tongbao|WD=Q8192962|lt=Yongle Tongbao}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|en|Kan'ei Tsūhō|WD=Q11457504|lt=Kan'ei Tsūhō}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)2.jpg |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572}} |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu no Ō|1=Takamuku no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28415200}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Uehara Nishimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|en|Yongle Tongbao|WD=Q8192962|lt=Yongle Tongbao}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|en|Kan'ei Tsūhō|WD=Q11457504|lt=Kan'ei Tsūhō}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano)]][[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano)]][[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Kawachinagano)]][[ja:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tsukaan Kofun |image = File:塚穴古墳 (河内長野市)2.jpg |caption = Full view of Tsukaan Kofun |alternate_name = |group = |location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572|1=Kawachinagano}} |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |coordinates = |iso = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Approx. 15 m (current state) |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |accompanying = |period = Unknown |buried = Unknown (possibly {{ill|Takamu no Ō|ja|高向王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu no Ō|1=Prince Takamuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28415200}}<ref name="sankei" />) |heritage = |cultural = |site_no = |remarks = Reconstructed during the early modern period |map = |map = |type = |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano|1=Kawachinagano}}, {{ill|Uehara Nishimachi|ja|長野地区 (河内長野市)#上原西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uehara Nishimachi|1=Q132044226|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132044226}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is currently shaped as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview == Initially constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, the tumulus was later dismantled and reconstructed, making it a unique example among kofun structures<ref name="bunkazai">Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998.</ref>.
Excavations by the Kawachinagano City Board of Education revealed cremated human remains inside a jar, alongside pottery, ceramics, and coins such as {{ill|Yongle Tongbao|en|Yongle Tongbao|WD=Q8192962|lt=Yongle Tongbao|1=Yongle Tongbao}} and {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|en|Kan'ei Tsūhō|WD=Q11457504|lt=Kan'ei Tsūhō|1=Kan'ei Tsūhō}}. Inscriptions on nearby stone pagodas and burial goods suggest that the chamber was reassembled during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and repurposed as a burial site<ref name="bunkazai" />.
== Legend == Local folklore recounts that the tumulus was dismantled during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}} under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to procure stones. It is said that attempts to move the large stones caused "supernatural events," leading workers to reconstruct the chamber in its original type<ref name="sankei">{{Cite news|lang=ja|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|title=Attempts to uncover the contents led to disasters? The strange legend of Tsukaan Kofun|newspaper=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Digital|date=September 13, 2017|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20240422030830/https://www.sankei.com/article/20170923-7OVK3BYOH5NBJMWGG2NYLQ7JHY/|archivedate=April 22, 2024}}</ref>. Wedge marks on the stones confirm attempts to split them during dismantling.
== References == * Uehara Archaeological Investigation Report (Tsukaan Kofun), Kawachinagano City Board of Education, 1998. * ''Kawachinagano City History,'' Vol. 1, 1994.<ref name="sankei" /> * Local intypeational signboard, Uehara Property District, 1997.
